what_manipulated: temporal sequence and presence of positive affect
what_measured: broad-minded coping strategy adoption and emotional well-being
experiment: Units completed self-report measures of affect and coping at two time points 5 weeks apart. Regression analyses assessed whether positive affect at time 1 predicted coping strategy at time 2, and vice versa, controlling for other variables. Mediational analysis examined serial reciprocal relationships. Analyses used prospective prediction to infer causal direction.

what_manipulated: transient spatial attention via peripheral precuing
what_measured: temporal resolution as measured by two-flash fusion threshold
experiment: Units completed a task measuring temporal resolution (the minimal time interval at which two light flashes are perceived as separate rather than merged) under conditions of manipulated transient spatial attention via peripheral precuing. The critical comparison was temporal resolution in attended versus unattended spatial locations. Changes in fusion thresholds were analyzed.

what_manipulated: marital status transitions (marriage to divorce) and marital duration
what_measured: arrest frequency and offending patterns
experiment: Monthly-level data across 13 years tracked status changes and arrest outcomes for a nationally representative sample. Within-individual multilevel models compared arrest rates during married periods versus following divorce, examining whether reductions in offending were enduring (stable across separation) or situational (reversing upon divorce). Moderating effects of marital duration were examined.

what_manipulated: racial/ethnic identity cues in written communication
what_measured: responsiveness of government bureaucrats to requests and tone of responses
experiment: Units (housing officials) received email requests for assistance varying in sender racial/ethnic identity (white, black, Hispanic). Response rates and email tone were compared across conditions. Field experiment measured whether officials responded and the language register of responses as functions of applicant identity.

what_manipulated: body mass index of visual stimuli presented to units
what_measured: estimated weight of bodies and just-noticeable difference in weight discrimination
experiment: Experiment 1: Units estimated weight of images varying in BMI; linear regression assessed underestimation magnitude as BMI increased. Experiment 2: Units compared pairs of bodies varying in BMI and discriminated which was heavier; analysis of variance tested whether the just-noticeable difference (minimum perceptual change) varied with stimulus BMI. Both experiments tested predictions derived from perceptual bias theories.

what_manipulated: stability vs. instability of causal relationships while holding overall causal strength constant
what_measured: endorsement of causal claims and willingness to use causal relationships as guides to action
experiment: Experiment 1: Units judged causal and explanatory claims for relationships varying in stability; regression assessed whether endorsement differed by stability, controlling for causal strength. Experiment 2: Manipulated generalization scope to test alternative explanations. Experiment 3: Measured action guidance judgments. Across studies, stability effects were isolated from confounds.

what_manipulated: not applicable (test development and validation study)
what_measured: global oral linguistic proficiency in a second language as measured by elicited imitation
experiment: Units completed an elicited imitation test, an oral narrative task, and a background questionnaire. Correlation and regression analyses examined whether test scores predict oral proficiency ratings, stratified by language ability level and learner type. Test validity was assessed via convergence with other proficiency measures.

what_manipulated: not specified (abstract too brief)
what_measured: not specified (abstract too brief)
experiment: Insufficient information in abstract to describe procedure, analysis, or comparison. Abstract provides only title and citation information.

what_manipulated: socioeconomic background (indexed by maternal education) as a grouping variable
what_measured: neural processing efficiency during selective attention and ability to filter irrelevant information
experiment: Young units listened to two narrative stories simultaneously while cued to attend to one; event-related brain potentials (ERP) were recorded to probe stimuli embedded in attended and unattended stories. Between-group analysis compared ERP components (selective attention effects) in units from higher versus lower socioeconomic backgrounds. Analysis focused on early (100 ms) filtering efficiency.

what_manipulated: absolute amount and proportion of resources given away by a puppet character
what_measured: ratings of niceness or fairness of the gift-giver
experiment: Units (children and adults) observed puppet characters distributing resources and rated which character was nicer. Within-subjects design crossed absolute amount given with proportion of available resources given. Analysis examined which cue (absolute vs. proportional) dominated judgments and how this changed with development. ANOVA and descriptive analysis of choice patterns were used.

what_manipulated: not applicable (philosophical literature review and reference list)
what_measured: not applicable (philosophical literature review and reference list)
experiment: Philosophical reference list and literature review; no empirical study design. Content appears to be a bibliography of philosophical works on intention and intentional action rather than a description of an experiment.

what_manipulated: implicit theory of intelligence (fixed vs. malleable) taught in Study 2; pre-existing implicit theory assessed in Study 1
what_measured: grade trajectories in mathematics and classroom motivation
experiment: Study 1: Longitudinal design tracking grades over two years; regression with implicit theory as predictor assessed linear trends in grades. Study 2: Randomized intervention: units assigned to receive either an incremental theory teaching session or control; post-intervention grades and motivation were compared between groups. Mediational analysis examined effects through learning goals, effort beliefs, and attributions.

what_manipulated: type of visual stimulus presented (social vs. other, inferred from design)
what_measured: hemodynamic activation (oxyhemoglobin concentration) in temporal lobe regions
experiment: Young units viewed visual stimuli while near infrared spectroscopy measured blood oxygenation in bilateral temporal lobes. Analysis identified regions showing significant changes in oxyhemoglobin relative to baseline. Localization of activation to specific temporal sites was examined to infer involvement in social brain networks.

what_manipulated: anchor values (self-generated vs. provided) and information search conditions
what_measured: magnitude of adjustment from anchor values and final estimates
experiment: Units made estimates of quantities under conditions where anchoring values were self-generated or provided, and where search for accurate information was encouraged or not. Regression analyses compared adjustment magnitudes (actual adjustment - insufficient adjustment) across conditions. Studies 1a-1b examined termination at plausible values; Studies 2a-2c tested whether encouraging deeper search reduced insufficient adjustment.

what_manipulated: numerical information presented in various formats and numeracy level of units as a measured grouping variable
what_measured: judgment and decision outcomes including susceptibility to framing effects
experiment: Four studies varied in task type but all involved numerical probability comprehension. Units varying in numeracy (measured via assessment) completed judgment or decision tasks; correlation and regression analyses examined whether numeracy predicted task performance and framing susceptibility, controlling for general intelligence. Between-group analyses compared high-numerate versus low-numerate units' use of numerical principles and affective responses.

what_manipulated: power level via priming manipulation in some studies; power as individual difference measured in correlational study
what_measured: perspective-taking accuracy, egocentric bias, and empathy-related judgments
experiment: Experiments 1-3: Units were primed with high vs. low power and then completed perspective-taking tasks (drawing orientation, knowledge adjustment, emotion recognition) measuring egocentric bias. Accuracy scores and response patterns were compared between power conditions. Additional study measured individual differences in power and correlated with perspective-taking measures. Mixed analysis of variance tested interaction effects of power condition.

what_manipulated: own outcome knowledge (whether unit knew event result) as a within-subject factor
what_measured: false-belief reasoning accuracy; ability to predict another unit's belief given contradictory information
experiment: Units watched an event and then reasoned about what another person would believe about that event, either knowing or not knowing the actual outcome. Accuracy on false-belief reasoning tasks was compared within-subjects across knowledge conditions. Analyses tested for curse-of-knowledge bias (own knowledge impairing reasoning about others' false beliefs). Plausibility ratings were examined as a mediator.

what_manipulated: beliefs attributed to an actor (true vs. false beliefs about object location)
what_measured: anticipatory eye gaze toward predicted action target
experiment: Young units watched a scene where an actor held a false belief about an object's location, then watched as the actor searched for the object. Eye-tracking recorded where units looked in anticipation of the actor's search. Gaze was directed to locations consistent with the actor's false belief rather than the true object location. Timing and direction of anticipatory looking were analyzed.

what_manipulated: conflict between response options presented during decision making
what_measured: trajectory and velocity of reaching movements toward response options
experiment: Units made evaluative judgments (e.g., whether murder is justified) by moving a computer mouse toward response options (agree/disagree). Reaching trajectories were recorded and analyzed for deflection toward non-chosen alternatives. On trials with high response conflict, trajectories were compared to low-conflict trials. Analysis examined whether simultaneous pull from multiple alternatives influenced motor execution rather than just final decision.

what_manipulated: presence or absence of response conflict on a current trial; presence of prospective memory cues
what_measured: goal shielding as indexed by forgetting of prospective memory cues and interference susceptibility
experiment: Units performed a spatial task while monitoring for rare prospective memory cues. Trials varied in whether they contained response conflicts. Primary analysis compared prospective memory cue detection on conflict versus nonconflict trials; cues were noticed less often on conflict trials. Secondary analysis examined whether this reflected heightened goal shielding and distraction inhibition on conflict trials themselves. Accuracy rates compared across conditions.
